bins.name: State jurisdiction must be preserved
bins.desc: The governors accept that LLA interconnection has national security dimensions but insist states must retain significant authority. They explicitly endorse FERC's existing distinctions protecting state authority over retail sales, siting, and load facilities not directly on the transmission system.
quotes: we strongly believe it is essential for states to retain significant jurisdiction in this area, we recognize that the timely interconnection of certain LLAs is a matter of national security and national economic wellbeing.
we believe that the limit as proposed would substantially impact traditional state jurisdiction over loads.
We look forward to further public participation on what characteristics and size best serve to implicate shared state and federal jurisdiction in this regard.

bins.name: 20 MW threshold too broad
bins.desc: The governors argue the 20 MW threshold as proposed would substantially pull loads that have traditionally been regulated by states into federal jurisdiction. They call for further public process to settle the right size and characteristics for triggering shared jurisdiction.
quotes: we believe that the limit as proposed would substantially impact traditional state jurisdiction over loads.
We look forward to further public participation on what characteristics and size best serve to implicate shared state and federal jurisdiction in this regard.

bins.name: Efficient study of hybrid facilities
bins.desc: The governors support requiring that hybrid generation-plus-load facilities be studied more efficiently, consistent with the bipartisan PJM governors' proposal.
quotes: It would also similarly require that hybrid facilities be studied more efficiently.

bins.name: Six-month study timeline standard
bins.desc: The governors support the position, shared with thirteen bipartisan governors, that interconnection requests taking longer than six months should not be deemed just and reasonable.
quotes: we also respectfully suggest that FERC examine interconnection study processes, and whether it should be considered not just and reasonable for an interconnection request to take longer than six months, as recently requested by thirteen governors of both parties.

bins.name: Improve study processes and forecasts
bins.desc: The governors call for national RTO load forecast standards to address inaccuracies caused by LLAs applying across multiple territories, and for a general fix to interconnection queue delays rather than only a targeted LLA solution.
quotes: It would also similarly require that hybrid facilities be studied more efficiently.
this proposal could be even more impactful if FERC were to develop national standards and guidelines for RTO LLA forecasts.
these forecasts have failed to account for the common practice of a single LLA applying for interconnection in multiple utility service territories.

some recent load forecasts have markedly exceeded real-world constraints on load construction, such as the available supply of semiconductor chips, risking significant overbuild and associated cost increases for consumers.

we also respectfully suggest that FERC examine interconnection study processes, and whether it should be considered not just and reasonable for an interconnection request to take longer than six months, as recently requested by thirteen governors of both parties.

a more general solution to interconnection queue delays is critical to enable the important investments in manufacturing, AI, and infrastructure that are needed for the United States to compete and win.

bins.name: LLAs bear upgrade costs
bins.desc: The governors support the framework's requirement that LLAs take responsibility for their network upgrade costs and note that inaccurate forecasts risk overbuild that drives up costs for consumers.
quotes: this framework would provide strong incentives for LLAs to invest in nearby new energy generation and storage resources, to curtail during tight market conditions, and to take responsibility for their network upgrade costs.

some recent load forecasts have markedly exceeded real-world constraints on load construction, such as the available supply of semiconductor chips, risking significant overbuild and associated cost increases for consumers.

bins.name: Incentives for nearby generation investment
bins.desc: The governors back the proposal's incentives for LLAs to invest in nearby new generation and storage resources and to curtail during tight market conditions.
quotes: this framework would provide strong incentives for LLAs to invest in nearby new energy generation and storage resources, to curtail during tight market conditions, and to take responsibility for their network upgrade costs.

bins.name: LLAs invest in proximate resources
bins.desc: The governors support requiring LLAs to invest in nearby energy generation and storage as part of the interconnection framework, mirroring the bipartisan PJM governors' proposal.
quotes: this framework would provide strong incentives for LLAs to invest in nearby new energy generation and storage resources, to curtail during tight market conditions, and to take responsibility for their network upgrade costs.

bins.name: PJM bipartisan governor alignment
bins.desc: The governors reference the bipartisan PJM governors' LLA proposal pending before the PJM Board of Managers and frame their federal comments as consistent with that regional effort.
quotes: a more general solution to interconnection queue delays is critical to enable the important investments in manufacturing, AI, and infrastructure that are needed for the United States to compete and win.

bins.name: National RTO forecast standards needed
bins.desc: The governors argue that FERC's national vantage point positions it to set uniform forecast standards that account for LLAs applying across multiple territories and for real-world supply constraints like semiconductor chip availability.
quotes: this proposal could be even more impactful if FERC were to develop national standards and guidelines for RTO LLA forecasts.
these forecasts have failed to account for the common practice of a single LLA applying for interconnection in multiple utility service territories.

some recent load forecasts have markedly exceeded real-world constraints on load construction, such as the available supply of semiconductor chips, risking significant overbuild and associated cost increases for consumers.

bins.name: Queue reform scope beyond LLAs
bins.desc: The governors argue that fixing interconnection delays only for generators paired with LLAs is insufficient; a general queue solution is needed to support manufacturing, AI, and infrastructure investment.
quotes: a more general solution to interconnection queue delays is critical to enable the important investments in manufacturing, AI, and infrastructure that are needed for the United States to compete and win.
